title: Averages and Mean
description: Understand how to calculate averages, work backward from averages, and solve average-based word problems commonly found in placement tests.

exercises.concept: Average = Sum of all terms ÷ Count of terms

exercises.test: The average age of a class is 20 years. After a teacher joins, the average becomes 22 years. Find the teacher's age.
exercises.solution: Teacher = 2 × N + 22 (where N = number of original students) - the teacher brought in 2 years for every student plus their own base of 22.
exercises.concept: When an average changes after adding a person: new person's value = old average + (change in average × count of originals)

exercises.test: The average of 6 numbers is 15. If one number is removed, the average becomes 13. Find the removed number.
Find the average of the first 100 natural numbers.
exercises.concept: For first n natural numbers: Average = (n + 1)/2. For an arithmetic progression: Average = (first + last)/2

exercises.test: The average of 5 cricket scores is: 45, 60, 80, 25, 90. How many runs are needed in the 6th match to bring the average to 65?
exercises.solution: Sum of 5 = 300. Target sum for 6 matches = 6 × 65 = 390. Runs needed = 390 - 300 = 90
exercises.concept: Target Sum = Target Average × Total Count. The runs needed = Target Sum - Current Sum.
